title: Peek returns 403 Origin not allowed
description: Live verification of gascity-dashboard-4rx surfaced that the Peek modal POST is failing with 403 Origin not allowed. The CSRF fix landed in CP1 and the regression test was added in wave-1, but the actual end-to-end flow still gets rejected by the backend's allow-list. Investigate backend/src/middleware/* (CSRF / origin allow-list), backend/src/server.ts (origin allow-list config), and vite.config.ts (changeOrigin proxy wiring). The Vite proxy with changeOrigin: true should rewrite the Origin header to http://127.0.0.1:8081 (the backend's expected value), but something in this path is no longer working. Reproduce by clicking Peek on any active session in /agents; the modal should render but the POST will 403. Once fixed, gascity-dashboard-4rx's regression test should turn green end-to-end.

title: Verify Peek modal end-to-end and add regression test
description: The CSRF / Vite changeOrigin fix landed in CP1 but was never reproduced live because /api/sessions kept timing out during the design pass. Open Agents, click Peek on any active session, confirm the modal renders opaque against the scrim and the POST succeeds (no 403). Add a Playwright regression test in scripts/snap-peek.mjs (already scaffolded) that asserts modal visible + at least one /api/sessions/.../peek 200.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=0f17d0d9a1a0787ac8f7cce5306b03a7c12864da on worktree-agent-ad534427865c0ff49 contains base
PLAN: Extend scripts/snap-peek.mjs into a regression test invocable via --test flag. Assertions: (1) backend reachable on :5174 — if not, exit 0 with skip message; (2) /agents loads and at least one Peek button exists — if not, exit 0 with skip (no live sessions); (3) click Peek, wait for dialog[aria-modal=true]; (4) read computed background-color of the inner panel (.bg-surface), parse rgba(), assert alpha === 1 (opaque per Modal.tsx contract); (5) capture network: assert at least one POST /api/sessions/*/peek returned 200 (no 403); (6) screenshot both themes for visual eyeball. Files: scripts/snap-peek.mjs (extend), docs/EXTENDING.md (add 'Running the Peek regression locally' note). Tests: this IS the test. Risks: subagent cannot run live; structural correctness only. Out of scope: backend changes.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) bg-surface resolves via CSS var to opaque OKLCH/hex — computed will report rgb() with implicit alpha 1; safe assertion. (b) Must distinguish scrim (outer dialog div, bg-fg/30) from panel (inner div, bg-surface) — select dialog's first child. (c) Backend-down branch must exit 0 (not throw) — wrap page.goto in try/catch with ECONNREFUSED check. (d) Existing snap-peek logs all /api/ 4xx+; need stricter check: POST /peek status === 200 at least once.
PROGRESS: extended scripts/snap-peek.mjs with --test mode. Assertions: modal panel opaque (alpha>=0.99 via getComputedStyle on dialog's firstElementChild), at least one POST /api/sessions/*/peek returns 200, modal becomes visible. Skip-not-fail when frontend unreachable or no sessions present. Helper parseAlpha unit-verified against rgb/rgba/hex cases. Added 'Running the Peek-modal regression locally' section to docs/EXTENDING.md. Manual verification required by operator (live click-test in browser) because subagent has no supervisor on :8372.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-ad534427865c0ff49 commit=5b87dc82e8b2497d20d30a7565fd3337bad9d8da files=scripts/snap-peek.mjs,docs/EXTENDING.md
REVIEW: 3 reviewers ran on the merged wave. HIGH found: parseAlpha did not handle oklch (the actual format Chromium returns for this codebase's bg-surface) — the test would have FAILED on a passing modal. Fixed in e43e390 with full modern-color-space support (21/21 test cases pass). MEDIUM also fixed: skip cascading assertions when modal does not open. MEDIUM cosmetic: skipped='no-backend' renamed to 'no-frontend' to match what the check actually tests.
close_reason: DONE: extended scripts/snap-peek.mjs into a --test-mode Playwright regression test. Assertions: modal visible, panel opaque (alpha >= 0.99 with oklch+modern-color-space support), at least one POST /api/sessions/<id>/peek returns 200. Skip-not-fail when frontend or sessions unavailable. Added docs/EXTENDING.md section. Wave Phase-4 review caught and fixed an oklch parsing gap. Live click-test verification by operator still required. BRANCH LEFT FOR USER: branch=wave-dash-wave-1-review commit=e43e39038c494077b8ce1f168470f307ae5834a7 — not yet merged into main; user must merge or this work is orphaned.

title: Investigate slow /api/sessions and /api/beads endpoints
description: Both endpoints intermittently time out (>10s). Surfaces as 'No sessions running' / 'Nothing on the queue right now' empty states in the dashboard even though the gc supervisor has real data. Likely a slow shell-out to gc CLI in the backend's gc-client.ts. Profile backend/src/routes/sessions.ts and backend/src/routes/beads.ts to find the hot path; consider caching, parallel fetch, or talking to the supervisor's HTTP API directly instead of shelling out.

title: Retire stale Wldc4rd/citadel paths in docs/EXTENDING.md
description: docs/EXTENDING.md lines 105-107 still reference 'tools/admin-dashboard/', 'clients/app/', and 'Thriva product app' — paths from the upstream Wldc4rd/citadel repo this project was extracted from. In the standalone repo the frontend lives at frontend/. The section needs to either be retitled and rewritten in standalone-repo terms (we don't have an upstream product app to import from anymore, so the 'why we don't import' framing no longer applies), or deleted outright. Surfaced by code-review during wave-dash-wave-1-review.

title: Retire residual 'Charlie' references in code comments and docs
description: After the standalone-codebase cleanup commit, 'Charlie' still appears in: backend/src/gc-client.ts (memory reference 'Charlie's corrected diagnosis'), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (multiple 'Charlie can identify...' references describing the operator), docs/SECURITY.md ('compose-from field is greyed when viewing-as ≠ Charlie'). Decide which to keep (genuine attribution / commit history references) and which to retire (runtime operator descriptions, replace with 'the operator' or 'stephanie').
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=0f17d0d9a1a0787ac8f7cce5306b03a7c12864da on worktree-agent-a2d6f9fc369e8d754 contains base
PLAN: 16 hits total. KEEP 4 (README.md:5+113, CLAUDE.md:23, AGENTS.md:23 — all genuine attribution to Charlie Coutts as upstream original author / MIT copyright holder). REPLACE 12: docs/SECURITY.md (2), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (7), backend/src/{gc-client.ts (2), routes/beads.ts (1), routes/git.ts (1), routes/mail-send.ts (1), routes/mail.ts (1), exec.ts (1)} → 'the operator' for generic descriptions; 'stephanie' specifically for the audit-log actor= references (matches actual audit.ts code which already uses actor: 'stephanie').
PLAN-REVIEW: Plan checks against CLAUDE.md operator alias = 'stephanie'. LICENSE not touched (out of scope, contractual). No commit history rewrite. Audit log doc strings in SECURITY.md:107 + ARCHITECTURE.md:90 are stale relative to code; updating to 'actor=stephanie' makes docs match the implementation rather than introducing a new claim. README/CLAUDE/AGENTS attribution lines preserve copyright context correctly. No scope creep — only Charlie references touched.
PROGRESS: 8 files edited — backend/src/gc-client.ts, backend/src/routes/{beads,git,mail-send,mail}.ts, backend/src/exec.ts, docs/SECURITY.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. Backend typecheck clean.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-a2d6f9fc369e8d754 commit=fb0977bd9d9a0ae7c94dbbf0333001b6b380aafc files=backend/src/exec.ts, backend/src/gc-client.ts, backend/src/routes/beads.ts, backend/src/routes/git.ts, backend/src/routes/mail-send.ts, backend/src/routes/mail.ts, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/SECURITY.md. (Also .beads/issues.jsonl with bd note state — harmless metadata.) Backend typecheck clean. KEEP-verified: README.md:5+113, CLAUDE.md:23, AGENTS.md:23 retain Charlie Coutts attribution per scope guidance. No frontend changes; no LICENSE touch.
REVIEW: 3 reviewers ran on the merged wave. Security: comment-only changes, no runtime behavior altered. docs/SECURITY.md actor=stephanie change matches backend/src/audit.ts. Pre-existing stale Wldc4rd/citadel paths in docs/EXTENDING.md (tools/admin-dashboard, clients/app, Thriva) noted as out-of-scope for this bead; filed as gascity-dashboard-28f.
close_reason: DONE: classified 16 Charlie occurrences. KEPT 4 (LICENSE, README credits, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md attribution lines). REPLACED 12 runtime descriptions across backend/src/{exec.ts, gc-client.ts, routes/beads.ts, routes/git.ts, routes/mail-send.ts, routes/mail.ts} (comment-only), docs/SECURITY.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (including ASCII process diagram label). Also fixed stale 'actor=charlie' doc lines to 'actor=stephanie' which now matches backend/src/audit.ts. title: Mobile and tablet responsive design
description: Per PRODUCT.md the target is MacBook Pro 16-inch primary plus occasional host console, so phone is not in scope. But the Header + Tables collapse fragile below ~960px. Decide if a basic tablet/phone layout is worth adding for emergency-debug-from-couch use, and if so, design the breakpoints (probably: collapse nav to a Menu disclosure under 768px, allow horizontal-scroll on tables, drop max-width to 100vw with side padding).
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=0f17d0d9a1a0787ac8f7cce5306b03a7c12864da on worktree-agent-afe89fed768ae2234 contains base
PLAN: Option B chosen. PRODUCT.md L9-12 specifies 'MacBook Pro... occasional host console directly' as the entire device surface. Anti-references reject density/feature-creep ('every dashboard tries to show everything'). No mention of phone, tablet, or remote-couch use. The bead's couch use case is speculative. SCOPE: stabilize 768-1280px (the fragile range that occurs naturally when the SSH-forwarded port is opened in a smaller browser window on the MacBook). APPROACH: (1) Layout/Header: reduce horizontal padding from px-8 to px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, reduce inter-nav gap (gap-7 -> gap-x-5 sm:gap-x-7) so the header row stops fighting at narrow widths; (2) Header inner: allow flex-wrap on the second row of bursts (already mostly flex). Keep header on a single row down to ~720px (5 nav + brand + toggle). Allow wrap only at narrower widths; (3) Tables: keep existing overflow-x-auto, no changes needed. FILES: frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx, frontend/src/components/Header.tsx, README.md (note device support). TESTS: typecheck only (no test suite for visual components in this repo). RISKS: changing padding affects all 5 routes; rhythm must remain. OUT OF SCOPE: hamburger nav, phone layout, horizontal-scroll shadow indicators, drawer mail layout.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) Padding change is global and affects every route — verified by reviewing PageHeader, Layout, all 5 routes; routes don't set their own outer padding, they sit inside Layout's main. Safe. (2) Header gap reduction must not change vertical rhythm — gap-x only, not gap-y. (3) Header could go single-row down to ~720px with px-4 + gap-x-5; below that the operator is in territory we are explicitly not supporting. We will NOT add flex-wrap to the nav; the bead description proposed it but per Option B we don't redesign sub-720, we just stop the fragile collapse in the supported range. (4) DESIGN.md compliance: no new colors, no cards, no second typeface, no em-dashes. The change is purely spacing tokens. (5) Greyscale Test: spacing has no color; untouched. (6) One Mark Rule: untouched. (7) Tabular figures: untouched (Table component already applies tnum). (8) README note: brief, factual, no scope expansion. No risks to design contract.
PROGRESS: (Phase 2) Layout main padding: px-8 -> px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8. Header outer: same padding + gap-x-6 lg:gap-x-8 gap-y-2 flex-wrap (was gap-8 no wrap). Header nav ul: gap-x-5 lg:gap-x-7 gap-y-1 flex-wrap (was gap-7 no wrap). README: added 'Supported device surface' paragraph after Quick start citing PRODUCT.md, layout stable from ~720px upward, phone explicitly out of scope. (Phase 3) Diff reviewed; minimal — 3 files of code + README. Typecheck clean (frontend). No DESIGN.md regressions: no new colors, no cards, no second typeface, no em-dashes in UI, .tnum on tables untouched.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-afe89fed768ae2234 commit=53a1d0072d3e27f811ee93aad22194cdefa0b369 files=frontend/src/components/Header.tsx,frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx,README.md
REVIEW: 3 reviewers ran on the merged wave. No issues — minimal Tailwind class changes, no DESIGN.md violations, no flex-wrap risk at supported widths.
close_reason: DONE: chose Option B (minimal fix, phone out of scope per PRODUCT.md L9-12). Layout.tsx + Header.tsx now use px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 responsive padding; Header gap split into gap-x/gap-y with flex-wrap as dormant insurance below 720px. README.md gains a 'Supported device surface' section. No phone/tablet redesign — that would be scope expansion against PRODUCT.md. BRANCH LEFT FOR USER: branch=wave-dash-wave-1-review commit=e43e39038c494077b8ce1f168470f307ae5834a7 — not yet merged into main; user must merge or this work is orphaned.
